title: IRS Impersonation Scam via Email
description: Scammers sending emails claiming to be from the IRS demanding immediate payment via gift cards to avoid arrest. The emails contain official-looking logos and fake case numbers.
summary: Fake IRS emails demanding gift card payments to avoid arrest.
suggested_action: Do not respond. The IRS never contacts via email. Report to phishing@irs.gov.
reason: Impersonates government agency demanding urgent payment.

title: Phishing Email: Fake Bank Security Alert
description: Users receiving emails claiming their bank account has been locked and they must verify credentials immediately via a link that leads to a spoofed login page.
summary: Fake bank security emails harvesting login credentials.
suggested_action: Do not click the link. Log in via your bank's official website directly.
reason: Classic credential harvesting via spoofed login page.

title: Local Package Theft Ring Reported
description: Multiple residents reporting package theft from doorsteps between 2pm-5pm on weekdays. Suspects using a white van. Several Ring camera captures shared with local PD.
summary: Package theft ring operating weekday afternoons in residential areas.
suggested_action: Schedule deliveries to require signature or use secure delivery lockers.
reason: Reported local criminal activity affecting community safety.

title: Medicare Scam Targeting Seniors
description: Callers claiming to be Medicare representatives offering free back braces and medical equipment in exchange for Medicare numbers. Used to commit insurance fraud.
summary: Medicare impersonators collecting insurance numbers from seniors.
suggested_action: Hang up immediately. Medicare never calls unsolicited. Report to 1-800-MEDICARE.
reason: Insurance fraud targeting vulnerable elderly population.

title: Data Breach: Popular Food Delivery App
description: A major food delivery platform confirmed a breach exposing names, emails, phone numbers, and partial payment card data of approximately 4.5 million users. Breach occurred via third-party vendor.
summary: Food delivery app breach exposed 4.5M users' personal and payment data.
suggested_action: Change your password immediately. Monitor statements for unauthorized charges. Enable 2FA.
reason: Confirmed data breach exposing sensitive personal and financial information.

title: Fake Tech Support Pop-Up Scam
description: Users reporting browser pop-ups claiming their computer is infected with viruses, displaying a fake Microsoft support number. Callers attempt to gain remote access and charge hundreds for fake repairs.
summary: Fake Microsoft pop-ups tricking users into paying for non-existent repairs.
suggested_action: Close the browser tab. Do not call the number. Run your actual antivirus software.
reason: Tech support scam using browser pop-ups to create urgency.

title: Phishing: Netflix Account Suspension
description: Emails claiming Netflix has suspended accounts due to payment issues, directing users to update billing info on a spoofed Netflix page that steals credit card numbers.
summary: Fake Netflix suspension emails stealing payment card information.
suggested_action: Do not click. Go to netflix.com directly to check your account status.
reason: Credential and payment phishing using trusted brand impersonation.

title: Neighborhood Break-In Spree
description: Seven homes broken into over the past two weeks in the Riverside area. Entry gained through unlocked back doors and windows. Police increased patrols but no arrests yet.
summary: Burglary spree targeting unlocked entry points in Riverside neighborhood.
suggested_action: Secure all doors and windows. Install motion sensors. Report suspicious activity to non-emergency line.
reason: Active local criminal pattern threatening residential safety.

title: Romance Scam on Dating Apps
description: Multiple victims reporting financial losses after building online relationships with fake profiles claiming to be military personnel stationed overseas. Eventually ask for money for emergency travel.
summary: Romance scammers posing as military personnel extracting money from victims.
suggested_action: Never send money to someone you haven't met in person. Reverse image search profile photos.
reason: Long-con romance fraud causing significant financial and emotional harm.

title: Credential Stuffing Attack on Local Credit Union
description: Community credit union reports unauthorized login attempts using stolen credentials from other breaches. Approximately 800 accounts accessed. Members urged to reset passwords immediately.
summary: Credit union hit by credential stuffing — 800 accounts compromised.
suggested_action: Reset your password immediately. Enable 2FA. Check recent transactions for unauthorized activity.
reason: Active attack using previously breached credentials against financial institution.

title: Smishing: Fake USPS Delivery Notification
description: Text messages claiming a USPS package couldn't be delivered, directing recipients to click a link to reschedule. Link leads to a fake USPS site collecting personal information.
summary: Fake USPS SMS messages harvesting personal data via spoofed website.
suggested_action: Do not click the link. Track packages only at usps.com directly.
reason: SMS phishing exploiting trusted postal service brand.

title: Gas Leak Reported Near Shopping District
description: Local fire department responding to gas leak near Main St shopping area. Three block radius cordoned off. Businesses evacuated. Utility company on scene. No injuries reported.
summary: Gas leak causing evacuation of three-block radius in shopping district.
suggested_action: Avoid the Main St area until all-clear is issued. Follow official fire department updates.
reason: Active public safety emergency requiring community awareness.

title: Social Security Number Harvesting Scam
description: Robocalls claiming Social Security numbers have been suspended due to suspicious activity, demanding victims call back to resolve or face arrest. Victims who call back are pressured to verify their SSN.
summary: Robocalls claiming SSN suspension to harvest Social Security numbers.
suggested_action: Hang up. SSA never suspends SSNs. Report to reportfraud.ftc.gov.
reason: Government impersonation designed to harvest sensitive identity information.

title: Phishing: Fake Amazon Order Confirmation
description: Emails with fake Amazon order confirmations for expensive items, urging recipients to click a cancellation link if they didn't place the order. Link leads to credential harvesting page.
summary: Fake Amazon order emails tricking users into visiting credential harvesting pages.
suggested_action: Check your real Amazon account directly at amazon.com. Do not click email links.
reason: Urgency-based phishing exploiting Amazon brand recognition.

title: Healthcare Provider Data Breach
description: Regional healthcare network confirms ransomware attack exposed patient records including names, DOBs, insurance information, and partial medical histories of 120,000 patients.
summary: Ransomware attack on healthcare network exposed 120,000 patient records.
suggested_action: Contact your healthcare provider. Monitor explanation of benefits for fraudulent claims. Consider credit freeze.
reason: Large-scale breach of sensitive medical and personal information.

title: Lottery Scam via WhatsApp
description: WhatsApp messages claiming recipients have won a large cash prize in an international lottery they never entered, requiring an upfront processing fee to claim winnings.
summary: Fake lottery win messages on WhatsApp demanding upfront processing fees.
suggested_action: Block and report the sender. You cannot win a lottery you did not enter.
reason: Advance-fee fraud using fake lottery winnings as bait.

title: Flooded Road Conditions: North Bridge
description: Heavy rainfall causing dangerous flooding on North Bridge Road. Two vehicles stalled overnight. Road closed by city engineers pending inspection. Alternate routes advised.
summary: North Bridge Road closed due to dangerous flood conditions.
suggested_action: Use Oak Street or Highway 9 as alternate routes. Never drive through standing water.
reason: Active infrastructure hazard affecting commuter safety.

title: Rental Scam on Facebook Marketplace
description: Fake rental listings offering below-market apartments requiring deposits before viewing. Scammers claim to be abroad and request wire transfers. No property access ever provided.
summary: Fake rental listings on Facebook Marketplace stealing security deposits.
suggested_action: Never wire money for property you haven't visited. Verify listings through official rental agencies.
reason: Real estate fraud exploiting housing demand to steal deposits.

title: Phishing: Microsoft 365 Password Expiry
description: Emails warning that Microsoft 365 passwords are about to expire, directing users to a fake Microsoft login page. Primarily targeting corporate employees to harvest work credentials.
summary: Fake Microsoft 365 password expiry emails targeting corporate credentials.
suggested_action: Go to portal.office.com directly. IT teams can verify password status through official channels.
reason: Corporate credential phishing using Microsoft brand impersonation.

title: Missing Child Alert: Westside Park
description: 8-year-old child reported missing near Westside Park playground at approximately 4:30pm. Last seen wearing a red jacket and blue jeans. Police actively searching the area.
summary: Child reported missing near Westside Park — police actively searching.
suggested_action: Call 911 if you see anyone matching description. Share this alert with neighbors in the area.
reason: Active child safety emergency requiring immediate community awareness.

title: Gift Card Draining Scam at Retail Stores
description: Criminals photographing gift card numbers in stores before they are activated, then draining balances once purchased. Particularly targeting Google Play and Amazon gift cards.
summary: Gift card numbers being photographed in stores and drained after purchase.
suggested_action: Buy gift cards from behind the counter when possible. Check packaging for tampering before purchasing.
reason: Physical retail fraud targeting gift card infrastructure.

title: Email Compromise: Local Business Invoices
description: Several local businesses reporting fraudulent invoices sent from compromised email accounts of known vendors, redirecting payments to attacker-controlled bank accounts.
summary: Compromised vendor emails sending fraudulent payment invoices to businesses.
suggested_action: Verify all invoice payment changes via a known phone number. Implement multi-person payment approval.
reason: Business email compromise causing direct financial losses.

title: Fake Charity Scam: Disaster Relief
description: Fraudulent charity websites and phone solicitations claiming to collect donations for recent disaster victims. Websites mimic legitimate charity names with slight URL variations.
summary: Fake disaster relief charities diverting donations meant for real victims.
suggested_action: Donate only through verified charities at give.org. Check charity ratings before donating.
reason: Charity fraud exploiting donor generosity during disaster events.

title: Suspicious Activity Near Elementary School
description: Parents reporting an unknown individual photographing children near Lincoln Elementary School entrance during pickup hours. School principal notified. Police conducting increased patrols.
summary: Unknown individual photographing children at school pickup — police investigating.
suggested_action: Escort children directly from school. Report any suspicious individuals to school security immediately.
reason: Potential child safety threat near school requiring community vigilance.

title: Bank Impersonator Fraud Calls
description: Callers claiming to be bank fraud departments warning of unauthorized charges, asking customers to confirm card numbers and PINs to verify identity and reverse the charges.
summary: Fake bank fraud calls harvesting card numbers and PINs from customers.
suggested_action: Hang up. Call the number on the back of your card directly. Banks never ask for PINs.
reason: Vishing attack impersonating bank fraud department to harvest financial credentials.

title: Phishing: Fake PayPal Security Alert
description: Emails claiming unusual login activity on PayPal accounts, urging users to verify identity by clicking a link. The spoofed PayPal page captures login credentials and 2FA codes.
summary: Fake PayPal security emails capturing credentials and 2FA codes.
suggested_action: Go to paypal.com directly. Enable security keys instead of SMS 2FA.
reason: Real-time phishing capturing both password and 2FA bypass codes.

title: ATM Skimmer Devices Found
description: Police discovered card skimmer devices on three ATMs in the Harbor district. Devices were recording card data and PINs via a pinhole camera. Affected ATMs have been secured.
summary: Card skimmers found on Harbor district ATMs — card data and PINs captured.
suggested_action: Check your bank statements if you used Harbor ATMs in the past month. Request a new card if concerned.
reason: Physical data breach device capturing financial credentials at ATMs.

title: Investment Scam: Crypto Trading Platform
description: Fraudulent cryptocurrency trading platform promising guaranteed 40% monthly returns. Victims able to make small initial withdrawals to build trust before larger investments are locked.
summary: Fake crypto platform promising guaranteed returns — classic pig butchering scam.
suggested_action: No investment guarantees returns. Report to SEC at sec.gov/tcr. Do not invest more to recover losses.
reason: Pig butchering investment fraud using trust-building withdrawal tactics.

title: Wildfire Smoke Air Quality Warning
description: Air quality index reaching hazardous levels in the valley region due to nearby wildfire. Health officials advising vulnerable populations — elderly, children, those with respiratory conditions — to stay indoors.
summary: Hazardous air quality from wildfire smoke — stay indoors advisory issued.
suggested_action: Stay indoors with windows closed. Use air purifiers if available. Wear N95 mask if going out.
reason: Environmental health emergency affecting vulnerable community members.

title: Phishing: Fake Toll Road Unpaid Fees
description: SMS messages claiming unpaid toll fees with a small fine that will increase dramatically if not paid immediately via a provided link. Link leads to payment card data theft.
summary: Fake toll fee SMS messages stealing payment card information.
suggested_action: Check tolls only via your official state toll authority website. Ignore and delete this message.
reason: Urgency-based smishing exploiting toll road systems to steal payment data.

title: Utility Company Impersonation Scam
description: Callers claiming to be the electric company threatening immediate service disconnection unless payment is made via prepaid debit card within the hour. Creating extreme urgency to prevent verification.
summary: Fake utility company calls threatening disconnection to force immediate payment.
suggested_action: Hang up. Call your utility company's official number on your bill to verify any account issues.
reason: Utility impersonation using time pressure to prevent victim verification.

title: School District Employee Data Exposed
description: Local school district confirms a misconfigured cloud storage bucket exposed employee personal information including SSNs, salary data, and home addresses of approximately 3,200 staff members.
summary: School district cloud misconfiguration exposed SSNs of 3,200 staff members.
suggested_action: If you are district staff: freeze your credit, monitor for identity theft, enable fraud alerts.
reason: Accidental data exposure of highly sensitive employee information.

title: Job Offer Scam via LinkedIn
description: Fake recruiters on LinkedIn offering remote positions with high salaries. After fake interviews, victims asked to purchase equipment using their own funds and ship to a provided address. Checks received are fraudulent.
summary: Fake LinkedIn recruiters using check fraud to steal money via fake job offers.
suggested_action: Legitimate employers never ask you to purchase equipment. Verify company existence independently.
reason: Fake employment offer designed to steal money via check fraud.

title: Power Outage: Harbor District
description: Unplanned power outage affecting approximately 4,000 homes and businesses in the Harbor district. Utility crews on scene. Estimated restoration: 6-8 hours. Traffic signals offline at 12 intersections.
summary: Unplanned power outage affecting 4,000 Harbor district properties for up to 8 hours.
suggested_action: Use battery-powered lighting. Avoid opening refrigerators unnecessarily. Use caution at intersections with offline signals.
reason: Infrastructure failure creating safety hazards for thousands of residents.

title: Grandparent Scam via Phone
description: Elderly residents receiving calls from someone claiming to be their grandchild in legal trouble, followed by a fake lawyer demanding bail money via wire transfer or gift cards. Extremely convincing and urgent.
summary: Grandparent scam calls targeting elderly with fake emergency bail demands.
suggested_action: Always verify by calling your grandchild directly. Alert elderly family members. Never send bail money to unknown contacts.
reason: High-impact impersonation scam specifically targeting elderly victims.

title: QR Code Phishing at Public Parking
description: Fraudulent QR code stickers placed over legitimate payment QR codes at downtown parking meters. Scanning leads to a fake payment page that collects card information.
summary: Fake QR code stickers on parking meters stealing payment card data.
suggested_action: Check for sticker tampering before scanning. Use the official parking app or pay at the machine directly.
reason: Physical QR code replacement attack targeting payment infrastructure.

title: Deepfake Audio CEO Fraud
description: Local company lost significant funds after an employee received a call with AI-generated audio impersonating their CEO, instructing an urgent wire transfer to close a deal. Audio was indistinguishable from real voice.
summary: AI-generated CEO voice used to authorize fraudulent wire transfer at local company.
suggested_action: Establish out-of-band verification for all financial transfers. No voice-only approval for wire transfers.
reason: Emerging deepfake audio technology enabling sophisticated business fraud.

title: Fake Antivirus Software Scam
description: Pop-up ads claiming computers are infected, prompting installation of fake antivirus software that is itself malware. Software requests administrator access and installs keyloggers.
summary: Fake antivirus pop-ups installing keylogger malware on victim computers.
suggested_action: Never install software from pop-up ads. Use only antivirus from official vendor websites.
reason: Malware distribution disguised as security software.

title: Water Contamination Advisory: Northside
description: Water utility issuing boil-water advisory for Northside district following detection of elevated bacterial levels. Affects approximately 12,000 residents. Advisory expected to last 48-72 hours.
summary: Boil-water advisory for 12,000 Northside residents — bacterial contamination detected.
suggested_action: Boil all tap water for at least 1 minute before use. Use bottled water for drinking and cooking.
reason: Public health emergency affecting drinking water safety for thousands.

title: Phishing: Fake Google Account Recovery
description: Emails warning of unauthorized access to Google accounts, directing users to verify their recovery options via a link. The fake Google page harvests passwords and backup codes.
summary: Fake Google recovery emails stealing passwords and account backup codes.
suggested_action: Go to myaccount.google.com directly. Enable Google's Advanced Protection Program.
reason: Account takeover phishing targeting Gmail credentials and recovery codes.

title: Urgent: Password reset required for your email
description: You must verify your account immediately. Click the login link to confirm your credentials or your mailbox will be disabled.
summary: Suspicious password reset email asking for credentials.
suggested_action: Do not click links; change password via official site.
reason: Credential-harvesting language and urgency.

title: Neighborhood scam: Gift card payment requested
description: Caller claimed to be from a government office and demanded gift cards to avoid penalties. They threatened immediate action.
summary: Caller demanded gift cards under threat.
suggested_action: Hang up; report to local authorities; block the number.
reason: Gift card demand and threats are common scam signals.

title: CVE advisory: Update your router firmware
description: Security advisory mentions a vulnerability (CVE-2026-1234) that may allow remote code execution. Users should apply the vendor patch.
summary: Router vulnerability advisory recommends patching.
suggested_action: Check vendor site and update firmware; reboot router.
reason: Contains CVE identifier and patch guidance.
